items.title: U.K. Defence Secretary John Healey quits, says govt. isn't willing to spend enough on military
items.summary: Publication of the defence investment plan has been delayed amid reports of disagreement between the defence ministry and the Treasury

items.title: ‘Not an era of war’: In Bulgaria, Jaishankar calls for dialogue and diplomacy to resolve conflicts
items.summary: EAM Jaishankar is on a two-nation tour of Bulgaria and Finland aimed at advancing India's engagement with European partners and strengthening bilateral cooperation across sectors

items.title: West Asia war LIVE: Third ship with Indian crew attacked near Oman in four days
items.summary: All three missing Indian seafarers dead after U.S. strike on tanker off Oman coast; Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is “fully closed,” but the U.S. denies the claim

items.title: Canada introduces Bill to restrict social media access for under-16s
items.summary: Bill C-34, introduced by Canadian Minister Marc Miller, is the latest attempt by the government to create a legal framework to address online harms

items.title: Third ship with Indian crew attacked near Oman in four days
items.summary: Reports say there are 20 Indian seafarers onboard Jalveer

items.title: Indian-American arrested in California for allegedly defrauding bank of $100 million
items.summary: Mahender Makhijani has been living in the U.S. on a Green Card and faces a maximum sentence of 30 years in prison if convicted

items.title: Three missing Indian seafarers onboard MT Settebello confirmed dead: Sonowal
items.summary: Union Shipping Minister Sarbananda Sonowal said he has directed officials to ensure immediate repatriation of the rescued crew members and swift return of the mortal remains of the deceased for their final rites

items.title: Bangkok shrine bombing case 2015: Thailand sentences Chinese Uyghurs to death
items.summary: A Thai court convicted Yusufu Mieraili and Bilal Mohammed of premeditated and attempted murder for their role in planting a bomb at Erawan Shrine in Bangkok

items.title: West Asia war updates: Iran announces closure of Strait of Hormuz after U.S. attacks
items.summary: Two 'violating ships' attempting passage through Hormuz hit, Iranian media says; Iran's UN ambassador Amir Saeid Iravani rejected Trump's threat, saying "no sustainable deal can be reached through threats, intimidation, or the use of force."

items.title: U.S. military strikes ‘multiple targets’ in Iran in second day of renewed fire
items.summary: The U.S. Central Command issued a statement saying its forces targeted "Iranian military surveillance capabilities, communication systems and air defense sites across Iran"

items.title: Taiwan says its sovereignty cannot be ‘violated’ as China ends coast guard patrol
items.summary: The patrol ended ‌after China ‘inspected 198 passing vessels and rectified violations involving three ships’

items.title: ​Sport during a war: On the FIFA World Cup 2026
items.summary: The U.S. is giving the FIFA World Cup a bad name

items.title: Philippine town seeks immediate airlift of food to ease hunger in quake-hit villages
items.summary: Sarangani reported 20 dead from the quake, the highest toll from the affected provinces, mostly due to a landslide that buried houses in the coastal town of Glan

items.title: Police arrest several unruly fans outside NBA Finals venue in New York
items.summary: The events took place in midtown Manhattan, a few blocks from the famous sports arena hosting the National Basketball Association Finals, which was sealed off for the game by a large police presence

items.title: Two Indian seafarers dead, one missing after U.S. attack on ship off Oman, says sailors’ union
items.summary: India condemns attack, summons U.S. Embassy’s Deputy Chief of Mission Jason Meeks to lodge a “strong protest”

items.title: Ex-intelligence chief Sallay ‘directed’ 2019 Easter bombings, says Sri Lanka Minister
items.summary: Minister Ananda Wijepala said Major General Suresh Sallay, who was arrested in February on accusations of “aiding and abetting” the attack, had identified a Catholic church to be bombed

items.title: UNHCR says fewer people displaced worldwide in 2025 but long-term refugee crisis persists
items.summary: Most returns ‌were to six countries: the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Sudan, Syria, Afghanistan, ‌Ukraine ⁠and Myanmar

items.title: FBI seizes 13 websites allegedly used by China to target, recruit U.S. workers
items.summary: The 13 websites purported to be affiliated with consulting companies that advertised job openings for current and former holders of security clearances. But the companies were all fakes and the job postings were a sham, officials said

items.title: Iran says ‘will target’ any vessel traffic in Strait of Hormuz after U.S. strikes in latest escalation
items.summary: U.S. Central Command said in a social media post that the military is striking “multiple targets in Iran” and that it's being done “in response to Iran's unwarranted and continued aggression”.

items.title: Erdogan says Israel's attacks on Syria, Lebanon threaten Turkey too
items.summary: NATO member Turkey has been one of the fiercest critics of Israel’s assaults on Iran, Gaza, ‌and Lebanon, saying Israel was the biggest obstacle to regional peace. It has halted ‌all trade with Israel and called for measures ‌against ⁠it at international courts

items.title: Ukraine launches long-range strikes on military, energy sites in Russia
items.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said his country's forces struck several military and energy infrastructure sites, including a military factory that he said supplied components for Russian drones and missiles

items.title: Pakistan says carried out air strikes at terror hideouts in Afghanistan, 26 killed
items.summary: “Precise and calibrated strikes were carried out along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border areas on hideouts and safe havens of masterminds and planners belonging to Fitna Al Khwarij, killing 26 khawarijs,” says Information Minister

items.title: Israeli strikes on south Lebanon kill 12: medical source
items.summary: "The number of martyrs from the Israeli airstrikes in the town of Tayr Dibba is eight, and in Deir Qanun al-Nahr it is four," the source said, speaking on condition of anonymity

items.title: Tehran will ‘pay the price’, says Trump after U.S., Iran exchange strikes
items.summary: “We are gonna be attacking them, attacking them very hard,” Mr. Trump told reporters on Wednesday. “We hit them hard yesterday, and we are going to hit them again hard today”

items.title: Watch: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes in major escalation | Above the Fold | 10.06.2026
items.summary: From Iran’s retaliation against U.S. military facilities and escalating tensions in West Asia to the Congress’s battle over Meenakshi Natarajan’s Rajya Sabha nomination, fresh turmoil within the TMC, Mamata Banerjee’s meeting with Sonia Gandhi - Top stories of the day.

items.title: Travel bans and ICE crackdowns cast shadow on FIFA’s ‘inclusive’ World Cup
items.summary: Trump administration’s hardline immigration restrictions and travel bans draw significant scrutiny ahead of the upcoming international sporting event; amid a ‘climate of fear’, advocacy groups launch campaigns to protect fans and residents from potential enforcement actions near venues

items.title: Knife attack victim’s family calls for calm after anti-immigrant violence in Belfast
items.summary: The appeal came as a Sudanese man appeared in court charged with ⁠attempted murder, and as British Prime Minister Keir Starmer and politicians in Northern Ireland condemned the violence by “masked thugs” that had targeted ethnic minorities

items.title: Pakistan Army helicopter crashes in PoK, all personnel on board killed
items.summary: A board of inquiry has been ordered to ascertain the exact technical cause of the accident

items.title: Iran fires missiles at air base hosting U.S. forces in Jordan
items.summary: Iran launched attacks on sites in Bahrain and Kuwait, which both sounded alerts and fired air defences in response

items.title: U.S. and Iran launch airstrikes after Trump blamed Tehran for downing Army helicopter
items.summary: The U.S. Army Apache helicopter that crashed off the coast of Oman went down after colliding with an Iranian drone, according a U.S. official, who spoke on condition of anonymity to discuss an ongoing investigation.

items.title: Netanyahu to run for re-election, his party says, after Trump raises doubts
items.summary: In a brief statement, Mr. Netanyahu’s Likud Party said he would run in the election and, God willing, win. The election has not yet been formally announced but must ‌be held by October

items.title: Benjamin Netanyahu | The comeback ‘king’
items.summary: With solid support from Israel’s right-wing and orthodox sections and little regard for the critics of their policies, the Likud leader, whose coalition has won a majority in the legislative elections, is to continue to dominate the country’s politics and external policy

items.title: Water cut off for thousands of Iranians after U.S. strikes: Iranian state media
items.summary: The U.S. strikes damaged two reservoirs supplying the Bemani and Kouhestak areas of Sirik town and 20,000 residents of the region have lost access to safe drinking water

items.title: Taiwan fires rockets in China’s direction from a U.S.-supplied mobile launching system in drill
items.summary: The HIMARS was the centerpiece of the drill and after receiving a firing order, the vehicles maneuvered into position and launched their rockets with bright flashes within three minutes, demonstrating their mobility

items.title: Ukraine strikes Mariupol port ‘significantly limiting’ its use
items.summary: The port was used by the ‌enemy for military logistics, as well ​as the illegal export of Ukrainian grain, ⁠coal and metal to Russia

items.title: China, Taiwan spar over legality of coast guard patrols east of island
items.summary: China said ships were sent for a “special maritime operation” in waters east of Taiwan after announcements from Japan and the Philippines; Taiwan accused these ships of “harassing” commercial vessels

items.title: Johannesburg shooting: At least 12 killed, 9 injured in late-night mass shooting
items.summary: Police believe more than 10 suspects were dropped off in a minibus in an informal settlement in the Cleveland suburb of Johannesburg in South ⁠Africa

items.title: Starlink says talks with India remain productive as it awaits final clearance
items.summary: Starlink remains in active and productive discussions with the Government of India contrary to misleading stories based upon unsubstantiated claims, says VP of Starlink Business Operations

items.title: Hong Kong charges seven people, two firms over deadliest fire in decades
items.summary: The massive blaze, which engulfed seven of the eight high-rise apartment blocks at the Wang Fuk Court estate in November, prompted a months-long investigation in to the cause

items.title: U.S. urges Europe to step up travel measures to prevent spread of Ebola from Africa
items.summary: Marco Rubio, on June 9, called European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen to discuss U.S. and European coordination and response efforts to the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda

items.title: We'll see if communism outlives Dalai Lama or the other way around: Head of Tibetan govt-in-exile
items.summary: The Sikyong also said China has "never used the tools" that can win the hearts and trust of Tibetans

items.title: Indonesian military court jails four officers involved in acid attack on rights activist Andrie Yunus
items.summary: The ‌four ⁠officers were found guilty on charges of serious premeditated assault ⁠after attacking Andrie Yunus, a deputy coordinator with a rights group known as KontraS

items.title: NASA announces Artemis III crew; taps U.S. astronauts, Italian for mission with SpaceX, Blue Origin mooncraft
items.summary: Artemis III will test spacecraft dockings in Earth orbit; mission is due to launch late next year

items.title: Markets, streets deserted in different parts of PoK after JAAC’s protest call
items.summary: JAAC has been staging protests demanding subsidised flour and electricity. The group was outlawed on Friday (June 5, 2026) by authorities, citing concerns over public order and security

items.title: Trump says U.S. ‘must’ respond after Iran downs Apache helicopter
items.summary: In a statement, Mr. Trump said he had been informed, "that last night the Iranians shot down one of our highly sophisticated Apache Helicopters while patrolling over the Strait of Hormuz"

items.title: New and raw: On Nepal-India ties
items.summary: Nepal and India must avoid misspeaking on border issues

items.title: Death toll from Philippines earthquake rises to 46
items.summary: The 7.8-magnitude quake on June 8 brought down buildings, triggered landslides and set off tsunami warnings across a swathe of the southern island

items.title: Bill Gates faces questioning in U.S. Congress over Epstein ties
items.summary: Bill Gates was due to appear before the House Oversight Committee for a transcribed interview about the Jeffrey Epstein, who died in a New York jail cell in 2019

items.title: Oil rises nearly 1% as U.S. launches new strikes against Iran, supply tightens
items.summary: The U.S. military’s strikes on Iranian targets ‌followed after President Donald Trump vowed on Tuesday (june 9, 2026) to respond to the downing of a U.S. Apache attack helicopter overnight

items.title: Inclusion without abolition in China’s hukou system
items.summary: Hukou reform pushes inclusion of migrants in public services while retaining structural controls

items.title: China’s role in the international order
items.summary: As China kicks off its 15th five-year plan, the country will remain a key contributor to economic growth, open its doors wider and share opportunities with the rest of the world

items.title: U.S. House passes $70 billion Bill to fund immigration enforcement for 3 years
items.summary: The funding comes on top of the nearly $140 billion that the Republican-controlled Congress gave ICE and Customs and Border Protection last year as part of Mr. Trump's tax and spending cuts Bill.

items.title: West Asia war highlights: U.S. strikes Iran after Apache helicopter downing
items.summary: United States President Donald Trump on Tuesday, June 9, said that a U.S. military helicopter was shot down by Iran and that the United States "must" respond.

items.title: Iran FM says foreign forces face ‘constant risk’ of being caught in crossfire
items.summary: “To reduce risk, best solution is for them to leave,” Abbas Araghchi added, after U.S. President Donald Trump vowed a response following accusations that Iran shot down an Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz a day earlier.

items.title: Former Taliban commander gets 42 years in prison in killings of U.S. soldiers, journalists’ kidnappings
items.summary: In April 2025, Najibullah pleaded guilty to providing material support for acts of terrorism and conspiring to take hostages

items.title: Protesters torch vehicles, block roads over Belfast stabbing
items.summary: Northern Irish police and British authorities issued repeated calls for calm earlier on Tuesday (June 9) following the attack captured in a gruesome video that shocked the country.

items.title: Hold Pakistan accountable for crackdown in PoK: MEA urges international community
items.summary: The latest protest that started under the leadership of Jammu Kashmir Joint Awami Action Committee (JAAC) on June 5 however was triggered when local residents demanded subsidy on electricity and flour and restoration of civil liberties in Muzaffarabad, Rawalkot, and Mirpur.

items.title: Modi expected to meet Trump on the sidelines of the G7 summit in France
items.summary: The PM’s visit to France will take place in two segments, with the first segment stretching from June 13 to June 14, and the second taking place from June 16 to June 19; he will hold a meeting with Mr. Macron on June 14

items.title: A new phase in India-Nepal relations
items.summary: Nepal PM indicated that the boundary dispute is not one-sided and should be resolved through diplomatic channels; his remarks, amid objections over trade and pilgrimage routes and shifting political tone in Kathmandu, suggest a cautious move towards a more rational approach in India-Nepal relations

items.title: Blast from recovered shell kills seven Afghans: residents, police
items.summary: The provincial police issued a statement urging people not to touch munitions, and to instead tell the security forces if they find any
